title: rubus schleicheri subsp. polyacanthoides
intro: Rubus schleicheri subsp. polyacanthoides, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus schleicheri plant, a type of bramble or thorny shrub native to the Himalayan region. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing, reducing pain, and preventing infection. The health benefits of Rubus schleicheri subsp. polyacanthoides include its ability to help lower blood sugar levels, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves, stems, or roots, and are often consumed as teas, tinctures, or infusions.
